seoTitle: Albany vs Alexander Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Albany and Alexander Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Albany vs Alexander Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Albany and Alexander Heights.
heading: Albany vs Alexander Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Albany, Western Australia and Alexander Heights, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Albany has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Alexander Heights (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Albany or Alexander Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Albany has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Alexander Heights scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Albany scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Alexander Heights scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Albany or Alexander Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Albany has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
